verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, processed through the secretory pathway, and released into the extracellular space/prostatic fluid as a soluble enzyme. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable covalent or wash-resistant attachment to a cell-surface TM partner. Its primary biological role is as a secreted protease (activating PSA/KLK3). Serum/tissue fluid detection is of the soluble secreted form. No credible evidence places the protein body stably on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
